data.title: Jacobs Launches Flood IQ To Tie AI Flood Risk Into Resilience Story
data.description:
• Jacobs Solutions (NYSE:J) has launched Flood IQ, an AI-driven flood risk platform focused on global water and infrastructure adaptation.
  • The platform is designed to help cities and utilities anticipate, manage and recover from flooding events in real time.
  • Flood IQ targets climate risk in critical water infrastructure, with a global roll out aimed at supporting resilience planning across multiple regions.

Jacobs Solutions, traded as NYSE:J, is known for its engineering, consulting and infrastructure services. Flood IQ extends that expertise into AI enabled climate risk tools. For investors, the launch sits at the intersection of water infrastructure, climate adaptation and digital solutions, areas that are drawing increasing attention from governments and utilities.

Flood IQ may give Jacobs a way to participate more directly in long term resilience programs, as customers look for data rich planning tools. Readers watching NYSE:J can track how this platform is adopted, how it is integrated into existing projects and whether it opens incremental opportunities in global infrastructure modernization.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Jacobs Solutions by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Jacobs Solutions.

📰 Beyond the headline: 0 risks and 3 things going right for Jacobs Solutions that every investor should see.

Flood IQ sits neatly between Jacobs Solutions' traditional engineering work and its growing set of AI powered services. By turning water and drainage system data into real time decision support for cities and utilities, the platform points to a market where clients are looking for software like tools as much as project design. That matters for competitive positioning against large engineering peers such as AECOM, WSP and Tetra Tech, which are also moving into digital and data centric offerings. For readers, the key angle is not just the technology itself but how often Flood IQ is embedded into long term water and transport projects, where it could influence scope, pricing and follow on work.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Jacobs Solutions Narrative

  • Flood IQ aligns with the narrative that Jacobs is leaning into higher margin consulting and technology work tied to infrastructure modernization and climate adaptation.
  • Heavier reliance on AI based tools could challenge the narrative if public sector clients are slower than expected to adopt software subscriptions or data platforms in core contracts.
  • The narrative focuses heavily on data centers, defense and broad water projects, while this product specific flood platform may not yet be fully reflected as a separate driver of digital services revenue.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Jacobs Solutions to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Dependence on government and utility budgets for climate adaptation projects could slow Flood IQ adoption if funding priorities shift or projects are deferred.
  • ⚠️ Expanding AI and data platforms requires ongoing investment, and if client uptake is weaker than expected, returns on this spending could be pressured.
  • 🎁 Flood IQ gives Jacobs an additional way to participate in long duration resilience programs, potentially deepening relationships with water and transport agencies.
  • 🎁 Successful deployments could support the company's push toward higher margin, recurring digital services alongside traditional project work.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, watch how often Flood IQ is referenced in new water, transportation and coastal protection awards and whether Jacobs is bundling it with larger corridor or tunnel projects like its I 290 work. Adoption by multiple regions, not just one or two early users, would indicate that the platform is becoming part of standard resilience planning. It is also worth tracking how competitors describe their own flood risk and climate tools, as this will shape pricing power and contract terms across the sector.

data.title: Is US Foods Holding (USFD) Still Attractively Priced After Its Strong Share Price Run
data.description:

Approach 1: US Foods Holding Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model looks at the cash US Foods Holding is expected to generate in the future and discounts those cash flows back to today to estimate what the business might be worth now.

For US Foods Holding, the model here uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $999.5m. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates those estimates further out, resulting in projected Free Cash Flow of $1,891.0m in 2035, all in $.

After discounting this stream of cash flows back to today, the DCF model suggests an intrinsic value of about $157.40 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $91.70, this implies the stock is trading at a 41.7% discount to the model’s estimate of fair value, which points to a meaningful gap between price and this cash flow based valuation.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for US Foods Holding.

Approach 2: US Foods Holding Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to connect what you pay for each share with the earnings that back it. It lets you compare the price you are paying today with what the business is currently earning, which is often the starting point for thinking about value.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risks. Higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk tends to line up with a lower multiple.

US Foods Holding currently trades on a P/E of 29.88x. The Consumer Retailing industry average is 20.71x, while the peer average for companies similar to US Foods Holding is 31.18x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for US Foods Holding is 31.19x, which is a proprietary estimate of the P/E that might be appropriate given factors such as earnings growth, margins, industry, market cap and risk profile. This tailored Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple comparison with industry or peer averages because it adjusts for these company specific inputs. data.description:

  • Recent coverage around General Motors has centered on ongoing execution in core auto manufacturing, its positioning in the US market, and broader discussions about how legacy automakers are approaching electrification and software. These themes help frame how investors are thinking about both the opportunities and the risks reflected in the share price.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, General Motors currently has a valuation score of 4 out of 6. This sets up a closer look at how different valuation approaches line up today and hints at an even richer way to think about valuation that will come at the end of this article.

General Motors delivered 76.8% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Auto industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: General Motors Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For General Motors, the latest twelve month Free Cash Flow sits at about $16.29b. Analysts provide explicit forecasts out to 2027, where Free Cash Flow is estimated at $10.73b. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extends the projections using its own assumptions, reaching an estimated Free Cash Flow of $12.59b in 2035. Each year is discounted back to today using a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach.

Pulling these projections together, the model arrives at an intrinsic value of roughly $119.28 per share. Compared with the current share price around $79.05, the DCF output implies the stock trades at a 33.7% discount. On this model General Motors appears undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for General Motors.

Approach 2: General Motors Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It links directly to what many investors watch most closely, current profits, and is easy to compare with other stocks in the same space.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings appear. Higher growth and lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher uncertainty usually point to a lower one.

General Motors currently trades on a P/E of 22.41x. That sits above the Auto industry average of 19.34x, but below the peer group average of 41.61x. Simply Wall St also calculates a “Fair Ratio”, which is the P/E level its model suggests based on factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market value and risk. For General Motors, this Fair Ratio is 26.16x.

This company specific Fair Ratio can be more informative than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for the mix of growth, risk and profitability that is unique to General Motors. With the current 22.41x P/E sitting below the 26.16x Fair Ratio, the shares screen as undervalued on this metric.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical Q1 Highlights Recurring Strength And AI Driven Platform Usage
data.description:
• Intuitive Surgical (NasdaqGS:ISRG) reported strong Q1 2026 results with a significant beat on earnings and revenue expectations.
  • Procedure volumes rose 17%, with recurring revenue making up a substantial portion of total sales.
  • The company expanded its installed base of da Vinci and Ion systems and introduced new digital and AI-enabled features.
  • Additional FDA clearances supported the company’s position following recent recalls and regulatory scrutiny.

Intuitive Surgical focuses on robotic assisted surgery through its da Vinci and Ion platforms, which operate at the intersection of medical devices, software, and data. For investors watching the broader move toward minimally invasive procedures and digital tools in the operating room, Q1 2026 shows how these trends are contributing to greater use of the company’s installed systems and related services.

With procedure growth at 17% and recurring revenue forming a meaningful share of sales, the latest quarter reflects a business that relies heavily on ongoing usage rather than one-off hardware placements. The combination of system growth, AI-enabled features, and additional FDA clearances provides more concrete data points to monitor when assessing how Intuitive Surgical is positioning its platforms in light of demand and regulatory expectations.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Intuitive Surgical by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Intuitive Surgical.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Intuitive Surgical following this latest news.

For investors, Intuitive Surgical’s Q1 2026 report gives hard numbers behind the story of growing robotic assisted surgery. Revenue of US$2.77b, up from US$2.25b a year earlier, and net income of US$821.5m versus US$698.4m, show that higher procedure volumes and a larger installed base are feeding through to both the top and bottom line. Basic EPS from continuing operations moved from US$1.95 to US$2.31, while diluted EPS rose from US$1.92 to US$2.28, which indicates that earnings strength was not just optical from share count changes. With recurring revenue at 86% of total sales and procedure volumes up 17%, the quarter points to a business that is increasingly driven by ongoing instrument, accessory, and service usage rather than one off system sales.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Intuitive Surgical Narrative

  • The Q1 revenue and earnings, supported by 17% procedure growth and high recurring revenue, align with the narrative that expanding global adoption and higher system utilization are key drivers of long term earnings power.
  • Ongoing FDA recalls of da Vinci S/Si reusable instruments and stapler safety reviews push against the narrative’s assumption of smooth regulatory and payer support, since they introduce potential reputational and operational friction.
  • The step up in AI enabled and digital capabilities mentioned around Q1 is only partially reflected in the narrative’s focus on product and digital tools, and may not fully capture how these features could influence surgeon behavior and competitive positioning versus players like Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, and Stryker.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Intuitive Surgical to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ The series of voluntary recalls for certain da Vinci instruments and FDA review of curved tip staplers highlight product quality and regulatory risk that could influence hospital purchasing decisions or utilization over time.
  • ⚠️ Analysts cutting price targets while keeping positive or neutral ratings signals that valuation expectations and sector rotation risk remain live issues even when quarterly results are strong.
  • 🎁 Q1 revenue of US$2.77b and net income of US$821.5m, both above the prior year, alongside an EPS beat versus several consensus estimates, support the view that procedure growth and installed base expansion are feeding through to earnings.
  • 🎁 Recurring revenue at 86% of total sales, backed by 17% procedure growth and broad adoption of da Vinci and Ion platforms, underlines a usage based model that can provide more predictable cash flows than pure hardware cycles.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, investors may want to watch whether procedure growth and system placements remain broad based across regions and specialties, and whether recurring revenue continues to represent a similar share of total sales. The way hospitals respond to recalls of reusable instruments, and any further FDA communication on staplers or other products, will also be important for gauging regulatory risk. Finally, pay attention to how quickly AI enabled features and digital tools are adopted in operating rooms, and to how competitors such as Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, and Stryker position their own robotic platforms and digital ecosystems in response.

To stay up to date on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for Intuitive Surgical, head to the community page for Intuitive Surgical to follow the top community narratives.

data.title: Has Tower Semiconductor (TSEM) Run Too Far After Its Recent Share Price Surge?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering whether Tower Semiconductor's share price still makes sense after such a strong run, or if enthusiasm has carried it too far.
  • The stock last closed at US$216.72, with returns of 1.2% over 7 days, 32.4% over 30 days, 78.0% year to date, and a very large gain over the past year.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Tower Semiconductor as part of broader interest in semiconductor names. It has highlighted how sharply some share prices have moved and how investors are reassessing risk and reward in this segment. This context has helped put its rapid multi year gains and recent short term moves under a brighter spotlight for valuation focused investors.
  • Despite those returns, Tower Semiconductor currently has a valuation score of 0 out of 6. The next sections will break down what different valuation approaches say about that price and point to a more rounded way to think about value near the end of the article.

Tower Semiconductor scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Tower Semiconductor Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business might be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting them back to today using an appropriate rate. It is essentially a way of asking what those future dollars are worth in the present.

For Tower Semiconductor, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of about $73.5 million, but the projection path used here moves into positive territory over time. For example, the ten year projections include free cash flow of $842 million in 2028 and extend out to 2035, with each year discounted back to today to reflect risk and the time value of money.

When all those discounted cash flows are added together, the DCF model points to an estimated intrinsic value of about $193.81 per share. Compared with the recent share price of US$216.72, this implies the stock is about 11.8% overvalued on this set of assumptions.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Tower Semiconductor may be overvalued by 11.8%. Discover 58 high quality undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Tower Semiconductor.

Approach 2: Tower Semiconductor Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much you are paying for each dollar of earnings. It gives you a quick way to compare what the market is willing to pay for one company’s profits versus another’s.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on growth expectations and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually support a lower one.

Tower Semiconductor currently trades on a P/E of 110.82x. That sits well above the Semiconductor industry average of 48.04x and above the peer average of 52.68x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio framework estimates what a more appropriate P/E might be after considering factors such as earnings growth, profitability, industry, market cap and specific risks. For Tower Semiconductor, this Fair Ratio is 49.16x, which aims to give a more tailored anchor than broad peer or industry comparisons.

Compared with this Fair Ratio of 49.16x, the current P/E of 110.82x suggests the shares are trading at a premium to what the model implies.

Result: OVERVALUED
